mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in 60% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered months, or the signal fails to separate bull/bear outcomes with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad consumer demand contraction, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint decline periods yield positive 60-day returns in 65% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 2% annualized during periods of high inflation confirms robust supply-side capacity, supporting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to negative during high inflation periods, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio drops below 0.5 in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls indicate strengthening economic activity and higher corporate revenue projections, which consistently produce positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows zero predictive power for 5-day returns (Rank IC < 0.01) across multiple 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year CPI increase surpassing 4% forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in 70% of high-inflation months, or the threshold effect vanishes when controlling for rate expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling retail sales reveals inventory accumulation and weakening consumer demand, leading to earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to predict return direction with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in walk-forward validation, or positive returns dominate triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during periods of falling unemployment signals a supply shock rather than demand weakness, triggering immediate market panic and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power when unemployment is stable, or 5-day forward returns are positive in 60% of observed stagflationary episodes.
